Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to transform a raw Windows system activity log into a process mining event log This involves defining cases process instances standardizing activities process steps and preserving timestamps and relevant attributes The output needs to be a structured table followed by an explanation of the logic

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data Format CSV like text with Timestamp Action Type App Window and optional details Keys Direction From To etc Time Span 2024 12 11 08 59 50 to 09 08 15 approx 8 point 5 minutes Applications Microsoft Word Google Chrome Adobe Acrobat Microsoft Excel Windows Documents Quarterly Report docx Document1 docx Email Inbox Report Draft pdf Budget 2024 xlsx Actions FOCUS TYPING SAVE SWITCH CLICK SCROLL HIGHLIGHT CLOSE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Determine Case Identification Logic The challenge in process mining is defining a case Here options are proposed Single Case for the whole session Document Centric with one case per document and Task Centric with one case per logical work unit

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Option C Task Centric is the most robust for process mining It aligns with how analysts view cases in user activity logs However to keep it simple and strictly tied to the provided data without over interpreting I will define cases based on the Primary Artifact Window being worked on during a contiguous session

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait looking closer Document1 docx is worked on then left for Email then Excel then returned to Document1 docx Better Logic for Process Mining To make the log analyzable we should treat the Document Task as the Case ID

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Actually for standard process mining Usually a case is a process instance If I treat every document as a case I get 5 cases This allows me to analyze the lifecycle of a document Open Edit Save Close

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Prompt Instruction A case can represent a logical unit of user work such as editing a specific document handling a particular email Okay I will follow the instruction Each logical unit of work Document Email is a Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Mapping Events on Document1 docx to Case Document1 Events on Email Inbox to Case Email Events on Report Draft pdf to Case PDF Events on Budget 2024 xlsx to Case Excel Events on Quarterly Report docx to Case Quarterly Report

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Handling Switches A SWITCH event indicates a transition I will include the Switch as part of the destination case as the start activity entering the new work context

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait what about the SWITCH from Word to Chrome If Document1 is Case A and Email is Case B the Switch event is the boundary I will attribute the Switch to the Target Case to signify entering this context

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Actually looking at the log Document1 is worked on then Email then PDF then Excel then Document1 again then Quarterly Report

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Refined Plan I will use the Document Window Name as the primary key for the Case ID Case Document1 Case Email Case PDF Case Excel Case QuarterlyReport Document1 is visited twice It should be the same Case ID to show the full lifecycle

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Refine the Activity Names FOCUS to Open or Activate Window TYPING to Edit Content SAVE to Save Changes SWITCH to Switch Context CLICK to specific actions such as Open Email Start Reply Send Email SCROLL to Review Content HIGHLIGHT to Annotate Document CLOSE to Close Window

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Drafting the Event Log I need to map every row to a Case ID Quarterly Report events go to Case QR Document1 events go to Case Doc1 SWITCH Word to Chrome goes to Case Email Email events go to Case Email SWITCH Chrome to Acrobat goes to Case PDF PDF events go to Case PDF Excel focus and edits go to Case Excel SWITCH Excel to Word goes to Case Doc1 and later Quarterly Report focus and edits go to Case QR

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case QR should be one case not two The user opens it at the start leaves and comes back later It is the same document The gap represents other work This is valid in process mining interleaving

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction on Switch Events In process mining a Case ID must be present for every event If I assign a Switch event to the Destination Case it makes sense Start of that task Example SWITCH Word to Chrome belongs to Case Email and SWITCH Excel to Word belongs to Case Doc1

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Reviewing the Case Logic If I split by Document I get 5 distinct processes If I keep Document1 as one case it spans 09 00 to 09 07 This is the most analyst friendly way to see the lifecycle of a specific artifact
